data.title: Zedge signals momentum with 37% subscriber growth and launches DataSeeds.AI for new B2B revenue
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Zedge, Inc. (ZDGE) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Jonathan Reich, CEO, highlighted "Q3 marked a return to revenue growth for Zedge, highlighting the resilience of our business despite an ad market that saw some disruption due to TikTok's absence from the U.S. until mid-February, along with tariff-related uncertainty that caused macroeconomic volatility and -- and expected softness at GuruShots, where we scaled back user acquisition to focus on what GuruShots 2.0 will become." He emphasized the impact of the company’s global restructuring, which delivered $4 million in annualized gross cost savings, and noted that "the closure of our operations in Norway, the streamlining of the GuruShots team and the associated decrease in discretionary spend, especially in lowering GuruShots' marketing investment helped return us to profitability on a GAAP basis and boosted our profitability on a non-GAAP basis."
  • Reich announced the launch of DataSeeds.AI, describing it as "a B2B content marketplace that focuses on supplying enterprise customers in need of both on-demand and off-the-shelf data sets, initially focused on images, enriched with detailed metadata for AI training." He stated, "Last week, we signed our first agreement with a leading global AI technology company, validating both the market opportunity and the value of what we have to offer."
  • Reich also previewed the company’s expansion into AI-generated audio: "We are also about to roll out an AI audio generator as part of pAInt, our Gen AI creation suite that allows users to harness the power of AI to generate custom ring tones and notification sounds with simple text prompts."
  • CFO Yi Tsai stated, "Total revenue returned to growth in the third quarter, up 1.3% to $7.8 million. Revenue growth was restrained due to the partial quarter of TikTok returning to the advertising market and the continued expected declines at GuruShots."
  • Tsai noted, "Third quarter subscription revenue was up 13.4% from last year. And our net active subscriber growth trend continued to improve, up 37% year-over-year and sequentially for the eighth straight quarter."

Outlook

  • Reich indicated, "In Q4, the benefits to free cash flow should further improve as we anticipate a total of about $600,000 in restructuring-related severance and the final payout of the GuruShots retention pool. We expect to complete restructuring-related cash severance payments in the first half of fiscal year 2026, totaling approximately $200,000."
  • Tsai stated, "We expect these payments to materially decline in Q4 to about $600,000 and then be immaterial in FY '26, and -- which is when the full impact of our $4 million in annualized cost reduction initiatives should start to show through."
  • Reich described five core strategic priorities: AI innovation, creator community empowerment, user and subscription growth, GuruShots 2.0 development, and scaling DataSeeds.AI.

Financial Results

  • Tsai reported, "Total revenue returned to growth in the third quarter, up 1.3% to $7.8 million."
  • Subscription revenue increased by 13.4% year-over-year.
  • Net active subscriber growth was up 37% year-over-year and sequentially for the eighth straight quarter.
  • Deferred revenue reached $4.9 million.
  • Zedge Premium's gross transaction value grew 4% versus last year, while net revenue more than doubled in the quarter.
  • Average revenue per monthly active user was $0.10, up 33% from last year.
  • GuruShots revenue was down 45% from last year.
  • SG&A decreased by 6% to $6.3 million.
  • GAAP income from operations was $0.2 million, compared to negative $0.1 million last year.
  • GAAP net income and EPS were $0.2 million and $0.01, respectively.
  • Non-GAAP net income and EPS were $0.9 million and $0.06.
  • Cash flow from operations was $0.9 million and free cash flow was $0.8 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased 46% to $1.2 million.
  • The quarter ended with $20.4 million in cash and no debt, after repurchasing 219,000 shares.

Q&A

  • Allen Robert Klee, Maxim Group LLC: Asked about advertising revenue drivers and TikTok’s market impact. Reich said, "We pursue advertising as a comprehensive effort on our part. So it's kind of hard to answer that question with precision and a recurring theme that we consistently broadcast is that we've got an outstanding team focused on optimizing CPMs."
  • Klee asked for details on TikTok’s return and its timeline. Reich explained, "our quarter started February 1, that they were not in the market for the entire quarter because the ban was not lifted until mid-February."
  • Klee questioned monthly active user trends and stabilization efforts. Reich said, "We are working on stabilizing it. And the focus is really on the well-developed markets. We have seen some improvement... we continue to refine and optimize our user acquisition spend to bring on high-value users in those markets."
  • Klee inquired about Emojipedia’s new features. Reich responded, "The Emojipedia has been testing new features such as create your own emoji with AI or some mini emoji games."
  • On Zedge Premium and DataSeeds.AI, Reich highlighted the web channel expansion and new B2B opportunities, stating, "We've also made Zedge Premium content available on web and that opens up a new channel for customers." On DataSeeds.AI: "We are actively in the market building relationships with prospective customers."
  • Regarding the AI audio rollout, Reich explained, "We're going to be rolling it out on a limited basis first before the end of June and then we will scale... it will be a feature within the Zedge Marketplace."
  • Klee followed up on cost structure improvements. Reich emphasized, "Our cash flow has and will continue to be outstanding. The restructuring certainly is helping us."
  • Tsai detailed, "the tail end of the restructuring charges in Q4 would be about $325,000... after July, it will be gone in terms of charges, but some severance payment will be paid through the next 5 months after year-end... Q4 accounting for cash flow will be about $600,000 related to retention bonus and related to restructuring charges."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was positive, with Klee offering congratulations and expressing interest in the company’s strategic initiatives and new product launches.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, consistently emphasizing operational improvements and innovation; Reich stated, "we're confident that our performance marketing efforts and product road map are assisting us in achieving these goals."
  • Compared to last quarter, management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism to greater confidence, referencing tangible results from restructuring and new product launches, while analysts appeared more enthusiastic, focusing on growth drivers and new initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 marked a shift to revenue growth (up 1.3%) compared to a 10% decline in Q2.
  • Subscription revenue growth rose from 13% to 13.4% year-over-year, while net active subscriber growth accelerated from 22% to 37% year-over-year.
  • Q3 reported GAAP income from operations of $0.2 million versus a loss of $2.2 million in Q2; non-GAAP EPS swung from (-$0.01) to $0.06.
  • The restructuring’s cost savings became more visible, with SG&A falling 6% in Q3 after a 9% increase in Q2.
  • Management’s Q3 tone was more forward-looking and confident, focusing on innovation and the launch of DataSeeds.AI, contrasting with Q2’s emphasis on cost controls and market uncertainty.
  • Analysts shifted from concerns about ad market headwinds and GuruShots’ performance in Q2 to questions about new revenue streams and monetization strategies in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged continued softness at GuruShots and a year-over-year decline in GuruShots revenue due to reduced marketing investment.
  • There was discussion of macroeconomic volatility, tariff-related uncertainty, and ongoing challenges tied to advertising market dynamics, notably TikTok’s return timing.
  • MAU declined year-over-year, but management reiterated the focus on high LTV users rather than overall user growth, with Reich saying, "MAU stabilization remains a focus, but not at any cost."
  • Restructuring and severance payments will persist through the first half of FY 2026 but are expected to become immaterial thereafter.

Final Takeaway

Zedge’s third quarter marked a return to revenue and profitability growth, driven by cost reductions, a sharp focus on high-value users, and strong subscription momentum. The company advanced its AI strategy with new product launches such as the AI audio generator and initiated its B2B marketplace, DataSeeds.AI, already securing its first significant agreement. Management’s emphasis on innovation, operational efficiency, and new business models positions Zedge to pursue sustained growth and margin expansion as it moves through the remainder of fiscal 2025 and into 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Analysts see oil market likely unable to handle OPEC+ supply surge past summer
data.description:
The oil market can absorb OPEC's production increases through summer, but sustaining that pace risks leading to sharp price declines and a shift to contango, where future prices are higher than spot prices, Rystad Energy said Thursday in a new report.

"Continued unwinding at the accelerated rate is very unlikely," Rystad's Priya Walia wrote in the report. "The only way OPEC+'s increase is possible is either if there is significant disruption in supply somewhere or non-OPEC+'s growth stalls," adding that actual flows likely will be lower than the announced production increases.

Rystad forecasts Brent crude trading in the $65-$70/bbl range in the coming weeks.

Saxo Bank analyst Ole Hansen sees Brent crude unlikely to hold near $70/bbl beyond the peak summer demand season, as summer consumption is tightening supplies, with refiners striving to meet surging gasoline and diesel demand, but the short-term squeeze is expected to be offset by rising OPEC+ output.

"Crude oil markets remain caught in a tug-of-war between bullish near-term demand and rising geopolitical risks on one hand, and macroeconomic headwinds and rising OPEC+ production on the other," Hansen said in a note.

On Thursday, crude oil futures held on to most of the gains from the previous session that were the biggest YTD, remaining supported by the risk premium centered around Iran and fading expectations for a nuclear deal with the U.S.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for July delivery ended -0.1% to $68.04/bbl, and front-month August Brent crude (CO1:COM) closed -0.6% to $69.36/bbl, while front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for July delivery finished -0.4% to $3.492/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Oracle stock surge bumps Larry Ellison to world's second richest person
data.description:
• Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) shares surged more than 13% on Thursday, aided by strong guidance for fiscal 2026, which resulted in co-founder Larry Ellison becoming the second-richest person in the world.
  • Ellison is now worth $242B, behind only close friend and confidante Elon Musk, according to data provided by Forbes. Musk is worth $407B, largely due to his holdings in Tesla (TSLA) and SpaceX (SPACE). He is also the co-founder of xAI, which recently merged with the social network X, formerly known as Twitter. Musk finalized his purchase of Twitter for $44B in October 2022.
  • Ellison added $25B to his net worth on Thursday, surpassing Amazon (AMZN) founder Jeff Bezos and Meta (META) co-founder Mark Zuckerberg. Bezos and Zuckerberg are worth $228B and $239B, respectively, according to Forbes data.
  • The 80-year-old Ellison owns roughly 41% of Oracle, which he co-founded in 1977.

data.title: New York regulator approves 29 infrastructure projects valued at $636M
data.description:
The New York State Public Service Commission said Thursday it approved 29 infrastructure projects worth $636.2M, enabling 642 MW of electrification upgrades.

The projects were proposed by Consolidated Edison (NYSE:ED), National Grid's (NYSE:NGG) Niagara Mohawk Power Corporation, New York State Electric & Gas Corporation, and Rochester Gas and Electric Corporation to address existing and near-term infrastructure in support of transportation and building electrification.

Con Edison (NYSE:ED) will lead five projects, including a significant expansion at Zerega Avenue to enable fleet electrification and a redesign of the Hunt’s Point electrical system, adding 380 MW of capacity at a cost of nearly $440M.

National Grid (NYSE:NGG) will oversee 22 projects totaling $126B for 184.8 MW of new capacity in the form of mobile energy storage devices for near-term high-power charging in upstate New York, while NYSEG will implement a $37M industrial electrification project adding 30 MW, and a $33M project by RG&E will expand EV charging infrastructure by 47 MW.

"Taken together, these projects represent a major step in modernizing and expanding New York’s electrical system and will accelerate the state’s progress towards its decarbonization goals," the PSC said.

data.title: Kenvue considering selling some skin, beauty brands - report
data.description:
• Kenvue is weighing the sale of some its skin health and beauty brands.
  • Clean & Clear, Maui Moisture, and Neostrata are among the brands potentially being looked at, Reuters reported, citing sources. Bebe, a German baby care brand, and Japanese brand Dr.Ci:Labo could also be included.
  • Neutrogena and Aveeno, two of the larger brands in Kenvue's portfolio, are said to not be under consideration.
  • Sources told Reuters that the brands that could be sold comprise ~$500M in revenue.
  • Goldman Sachs is reportedly advising Kenvue on the potential divestment.

data.title: NextDecade finalizes $9B in contracts with Bechtel for Rio Grande LNG construction work
data.description:
NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) +3.7% post-market Thursday after saying it has finalized contracts with Bechtel to construct train 4 and train 5 at its Rio Grande LNG facility, with a combined value of ~$9B.

Under engineering, procurement, and construction contracts, NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) said it agreed to pay Bechtel ~$4.77B for work on Rio Grande LNG Train 4 and ~$4.32B for work on Rio Grande LNG Train 5.

NextDecade (NEXT) said it expects the pricing validity under the contracts to extend through September 15, and it aims to achieve a positive final investment decision on train 4 before the end of the pricing validity period for its EPC contract.

The company recently announced a 20-year, 2M metric tons/year LNG sale and purchase agreement with JERA for offtake from Train 5 and is seeking to commercialize an additional 2.5M tons/year under long-term LNG SPAs to support a positive FID on Train 5.

data.title: Elanco gains positive EMA opinion for Zenrelia for itching in dogs
data.description:
• The European Medicines Agency's Committee for Veterinary Medicinal Products has issued a positive opinion for Elanco Animal Health's (NYSE:ELAN) Zenrelia (ilunocitinib) for the treatment of pruritus (itching) associated with allergic dermatitis in dogs.
  • Elanco plans a launch after receiving formal marketing authorization with an eye toward Q3.
  • Zenrelia is already approved in the U.S., Canada, Japan, and Brazil. Approvals in Australia and the U.K. are expected later this year.

data.title: Neuralink files to raise $649M in financing
data.description:
• Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) founder Elon Musk's neurotechnology company, Neuralink, has filed for a $649M equity offering. 
  • An SEC filing dated Thursday indicates that the offering will not last more than a year. 
  • Neuralink designs brain-computer interfaces that can be surgically implanted. These implants allow the user to control electronic devices, such as smartphones or computers, with their thoughts alone. The technology is intended to help people with paralysis or other debilitating neurological conditions the ability to gain more autonomy and independence.
  • Musk also runs Starlink (STRLK), SpaceX (SPACE), xAI and X.

data.title: Gold shoots higher on simmering geopolitical tensions, weaker U.S. dollar
data.description:
Gold futures climbed towards the $3,400/oz level on Thursday, as geopolitical and trade tensions prompted a selloff in the U.S. dollar, which helped turn investors back towards favoring precious metals as a safe haven.

President Trump said the U.S. was moving personnel out of the Middle East because it "could be a dangerous place," and Iran's defense minister said on Wednesday that his country would retaliate if attacked by striking U.S. bases in the region.

"The longer the Iran situation continues to smolder, the greater safe haven flows into the shiny metal," Mizuho's Robert Yawger said in a note, adding that he anticipates gold will retest its record highs as long as the turmoil continues.

Trade-related headlines added to the mix, as Trump said that he was willing to extend the July 8 deadline for completing trade talks, but also that he is ready to impose unilateral tariffs within two weeks to multiple trading counterparts.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for June delivery (XAUUSD:CUR) finished +1.8% to $3,380.90/oz, its best settlement value since May 7 and fifth-highest close in history, while front-month Comex silver for June delivery (XAGUSD:CUR) edged up by +0.1% to $36.213/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (SPLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: XTI Aerospace launches public offering of up to 3.96M shares, pre-funded warrants
data.description:
• XTI Aerospace (NASDAQ:XTIA) is conducting a firm commitment public offering of up to 3.96M shares of common stock.
  • Shares are priced at $5.05 each.
  • Purchasers exceeding 4.99% (or 9.99%, by election) ownership may instead receive pre-funded warrants.
  • Up to 3.96M pre-funded warrants will be offered.
  • Pre-funded warrants are immediately exercisable and remain so until fully exercised.
  • The offering includes the underlying shares issuable upon exercise of the warrants.
  • Source: SEC filing
